notes: Was able to launch it via lutris (use .exe with dx11 in it). Game loads and everything seems to work, but performance in pretty bad: it stutters A LOT and it seems to be an issue on more powerful machines as well, so I guess we need to wait for that to be fixed.
title: Control

notes: Just like quantum break this game doesn't handle PC very well. You will have to comprimise on your graphics quality but generally the game is fully playable.
title: Control

notes: After creating a Lutris profile to run the DX11 version of the game executable (as suggested by other poster) the game runs fine, although framerate is not great - 30-50FPS, at times lower or higher. I would consider the game playable so I copied my saves from Windows and am continuing to play the game on Linux.
title: Control

notes: The game runs out of the box with proton (added as an external program, DX11 executable), without bugs or crashes. Performance is really bad though, I have to set the quality to low to reach 60fps.
title: Control

notes: Performance is not great, but it's playable. Shader compliation stuterring also isn't felt too often, but areas with heavy volumetric lightning just kill the framerate.
title: Control

notes: runs decently out of the box. i get about 40-50 fps with the same settings i got 60 fps in win 7. however i couldnt'get my xbox360 gamepad to work at all (works in other games)
